original_hypothesis.title: BondingCurve.setCredContract Not Called in Deployment Script Causes All Trades to Fail
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In Deploy.s.sol, bondingCurve.setCredContract(address(credProxy)) is commented out. Without this call, BondingCurve.credContract remains address(0), causing all calls to getPriceData to revert when accessing credContract.getCreatorRoyalty() and credContract.protocolFeePercent().
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: BondingCurve._getProtocolFee calls credContract.protocolFeePercent(). If credContract is address(0), this is a call to the zero address which will revert. getPriceData is called by _handleTrade for every buy/sell, meaning ALL share trading is non-functional until setCredContract is called.
deployment_assumptions: setCredContract must be called post-deployment before any trade

proof_of_signal.pattern: credContract.protocolFeePercent() and credContract.getCreatorRoyalty() called on potentially unset address

preconditions: User attempts to create a cred or trade shares

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Critical dependency not initialized in deployment script
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: credContract = address(0) causes revert on any external call to it
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No check in constructor/initialize that credContract is set; no zero-address fallback

analysis_questions: Is setCredContract called in a separate post-deployment step?
Could Cred.createCred revert during the initial buyShareCred if bonding curve fails?
Is there a FixedPriceBondingCurve that doesn't need credContract?
validation_requirements: Deploy without calling setCredContract and attempt a trade

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not an attack per se, but a deployment failure: all share trading is non-functional until owner calls setCredContract. Any cred creation attempt reverts because the initial buyShareCred call fails.
prerequisites: Deployment follows the commented-out script exactly

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete protocol DoS until configuration fix
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users attempting to create creds or trade shares

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No integration test with actual deployment script sequence

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: First trade attempt would reveal the issue

validation_result.impact_summary: All share trading (buy/sell) and cred creation via BondingCurve reverts because credContract is address(0), causing a complete protocol DoS until the owner manually calls setCredContract.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis correctly identifies that Deploy.s.sol has `bondingCurve.setCredContract(address(credProxy))` commented out. BondingCurve.sol declares `ICred public credContract` which defaults to address(0). Every call to `getPriceData` immediately invokes `credContract.getCreatorRoyalty(credId_)` — a CALL to address(0). Since address(0) has no code, the EVM returns empty returndata, but Solidity 0.8.25 expects ABI-encoded return data for `(uint16, uint16)`. The empty returndata causes an ABI decoding revert. Similarly, `_getProtocolFee` calls `credContract.protocolFeePercent()` which also reverts. Since `getPriceData` is called by `_handleTrade` for every single buy/sell, and `_handleTrade` is called during `createCred` (via the initial `buyShareCred(credIdCounter, 1, 0)` call), ALL protocol functionality that touches the BondingCurve is broken from deployment. The `getBuyPriceAfterFee` and `getSellPriceAfterFee` view functions also call `credContract` and would revert. There is no zero-address guard in any of these functions.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: BondingCurve.sol `getPriceData` directly calls `credContract.getCreatorRoyalty(credId_)` as its first operation. BondingCurve.sol `_getProtocolFee` calls `credContract.protocolFeePercent()`. Deploy.s.sol explicitly has `// bondingCurve.setCredContract(address(credProxy));` commented out. BondingCurve constructor only sets owner, never initializes credContract.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: credContract.getCreatorRoyalty(credId_) is called when credContract is address(0). This CALL to address(0) succeeds with empty returndata, but ABI decoding expects (uint16, uint16) and reverts on empty data.
credContract.protocolFeePercent() will revert when credContract is address(0) for the same reason as above.
Constructor does not set credContract. The only way to set it is via setCredContract(), which is commented out in deployment.
Both setCredContract calls are commented out, meaning deployment leaves credContract as address(0).

calling_sequence: 1. User calls Cred.createCred() to create a new cred
3. _createCredInternal() calls buyShareCred(credIdCounter, 1, 0) for the initial share purchase
6. BondingCurve.getPriceData() executes: (uint16 buyShareRoyalty, uint16 sellShareRoyalty) = credContract.getCreatorRoyalty(credId_)
7. credContract is address(0), CALL to address(0) returns empty data, ABI decode reverts
8. Entire transaction reverts — cred creation fails
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path from any user action (createCred, buyShareCred, sellShareCred, batchBuyShareCred, batchSellShareCred) always passes through _handleTrade -> IBondingCurve.getPriceData -> credContract.getCreatorRoyalty(). With credContract = address(0), this call always reverts. The same applies to view functions getBuyPriceAfterFee and getSellPriceAfterFee which call _getProtocolFee and _getCreatorFee, both of which access credContract.

deployment_assumptions_checked: setCredContract must be called post-deployment before any trade
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deploy.s.sol explicitly shows `// bondingCurve.setCredContract(address(credProxy));` commented out. A second commented line `// BondingCurve(bondingCurveAddress).setCredContract(address(credProxy));` also exists. No other script in the provided deployment scripts calls setCredContract. The BondingCurve is deployed with owner=oji3 (not deployer), so only oji3 can call setCredContract post-deployment.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack scenario but a deployment misconfiguration that results in complete protocol DoS. If the deployment script Deploy.s.sol is executed as-is, the BondingCurve will have credContract=address(0). Any attempt to create a cred or trade shares will revert. The protocol is completely non-functional for share trading until the owner (oji3) manually calls setCredContract. The fix is trivial (uncomment the line), but the code also lacks a zero-address guard on credContract usage.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Complete denial of service for all share trading functionality (buyShareCred, sellShareCred, batchBuyShareCred, batchSellShareCred) and cred creation (createCred). The protocol's core functionality is entirely broken until the owner calls setCredContract. Recovery is possible by having the owner call setCredContract(credProxyAddress). Additionally, all price query view functions (getBuyPriceAfterFee, getSellPriceAfterFee) also revert, meaning frontends cannot even display prices.
recommended_mitigations: Add a zero-address check in BondingCurve.getPriceData: require(address(credContract) != address(0), 'credContract not set')
Add a zero-address check in BondingCurve._getProtocolFee for credContract
Consider setting credContract in BondingCurve constructor rather than via a separate setter
Add integration tests that execute the full deployment script and then attempt a cred creation to catch this class of misconfiguration
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy all contracts using Deploy.s.sol as-is (with setCredContract commented out). 2. Verify bondingCurve.getCredContract() returns address(0). 3. Attempt to call Cred.createCred() with valid parameters — expect revert. 4. Attempt to call Cred.buyShareCred() on any credId — expect revert. 5. Call bondingCurve.setCredContract(credProxyAddress) as owner. 6. Retry createCred and buyShareCred — expect success.
uncertainties: Whether the team has a separate post-deployment configuration script not included in the provided files
Whether there is an automated deployment pipeline that includes the setCredContract call outside of Foundry scripts
additional_testing_needed: Full end-to-end deployment test that runs Deploy.s.sol and attempts basic protocol operations
Verify that the BondingCurve owner (oji3) has the capability to call setCredContract in production
Check if there are any monitoring or alerting systems that would detect this misconfiguration before users attempt trades
